One-Liner Description: AI platform for clinical development focused on protocol design, document authoring, and workflow automation to accelerate study startup.
Target Segment: Biopharma clinical development teams, study design teams, clinical operations, and medical device sponsors designing complex studies
Key Capabilities: Study design optimization with real-time insights; document authoring with ICH M11-compliant protocol drafts; workflow automation; one-click push to Veeva EDC; API and USDM JSON integrations; site budget automation; protocol digitization and downstream study startup connectivity
Weaknesses: More focused on protocol design and study startup than broader trial execution/data capture; pricing not public; may be strongest for enterprise teams with structured downstream systems and standards libraries
Funding Stage: Series B/late growth private; raised $15M Series A in 2021 and $20M in 2023 led by General Catalyst, with Section 32, Polaris, Zetta Ventures, and Northpond participating

One-Liner Description: Clinical-grade AI for study startup; auto-drafts protocols, ICFs, IBs; Microsoft Word Add-in integration. (web)
Target Segment: CROs, biotech, pharma – Clinical Documentation teams (web)
Key Capabilities: AI protocol drafting, ICF generation, IB drafting, training materials, site packs, Microsoft Word Add-in, GxP compliance focus (web)
Weaknesses: Founded 2025, unfunded, ~10 employees; no named customers; unproven product-market fit (web)
Funding Stage: Unfunded/bootstrapped; founded 2025; 8 live trials (Phase 1-2); 4 customers; ICON POC €100-200K (from VC doc) (web)
Source: cori-clinical.com; pitchbook.com; tracxn.com; VC insights doc

One-Liner Description: AI platform for protocol drafting and regulatory submissions; database of 800K trials; vision to be the OS for clinical trials.
Target Segment: Biopharma sponsors, MedTech companies – R&D, Clinical Ops, Regulatory Affairs (web)
Key Capabilities: Protocol AI (NLP/LLM protocol generation), 800K linked trial database, Deduklick (96.2% recall deduplication), Study Engager (patient engagement tool), evidence-based design recommendations (web)
Weaknesses: Currently limited to protocol design; broader vision not yet delivered; seed-stage with ~11 employees; no disclosed enterprise pricing (web)
Funding Stage: Seed stage; Debiopharm Innovation Fund (Apr 2024); €5M revenue, expecting €10M by EOY; €17M multi-year Novartis contract (web)
Source: risklick.ch; crunchbase.com; globenewswire.com (Jan 13, 2026); debiopharm.com; fiercebiotech.com; startupticker.ch

One-Liner Description: Pharma intelligence platform (Norstella subsidiary) with Trialtrove, Sitetrove, Pharmaprojects; $5B valuation post-merger. (web)
Target Segment: Large pharma, biotech, CROs, academic medical centers – Clinical Development, Commercial Intelligence (web)
Key Capabilities: Trialtrove (60K+ sources), Sitetrove (500K investigators, 190K sites, 185 countries), Pharmaprojects (100K drug profiles), Ella AI chatbot, Protocol SmartDesign, Investigator SmartSelect, Cohort Builder, AI Importer for TrialScope, NorstellaLinQ (74B data points) (web)
Weaknesses: Siloed modules; descriptive dashboards not generative AI; Glassdoor: 2.6/5 culture rating, 15% would recommend (web); no end-to-end protocol drafting (partnered with Risklick for this)
Pricing: Multiple module subscriptions; 12-month standard, multi-year encouraged; custom enterprise pricing (web)
Funding Stage: Part of Norstella ($5B valuation); owned by Warburg Pincus + Mubadala; 1,600+ employees across 5 brands (web)

One-Liner Description: Generative AI (NLG) for regulatory document automation; 150+ clinical trials; backed by Eli Lilly and Novartis. (web)
Target Segment: Pharma/biotech – Medical Writers, Regulatory Affairs teams (web)
Key Capabilities: Yseop Copilot (composite AI: LLM + business logic + domain rules), CSR automation, patient narrative generation (4hrs→4sec), SCS documents, CMC submissions, eCTD integration (web)
Weaknesses: Focused on document generation only; no trial design, site selection, or feasibility; no integrated workspace spanning full trial lifecycle
Funding Stage: €10M growth funding from Claret Capital Partners (Sept 2025); strategic investors: Eli Lilly, Novartis (dRx Capital), NextStage AM, Wille Finance (web)

One-Liner Description: Oncology-focused AI and real-world data platform for translational medicine, clinical trials, clinical development, imaging/diagnostics, and commercial insights; offers generative and agentic AI via CARAai and Precision Suite.
Target Segment: Life sciences enterprises, healthcare providers, oncology researchers, biopharma sponsors – especially oncology clinical development and commercial teams
Key Capabilities: Precision Trials for feasibility, protocol optimization, site selection, and patient recruitment forecasting; Precision Explorer for RWE analysis; Precision GTM; Precision 360; Digital Trial Solutions; SmartLinQ/CancerLinQ real-time clinical insights and trial screening support; oncology-focused multi-modal RWD and generative/agentic AI
Weaknesses: Broad oncology RWD/analytics platform rather than end-to-end clinical documentation workflow; appears strongest in oncology, so less generalizable across all therapeutic areas; enterprise-scale platform likely heavier and more data-dependent than focused workflow startups
Pricing: Not publicly disclosed; enterprise SaaS/data platform pricing
Funding Stage: Series C; $150M raised in 2022 at $1.9B valuation from Sixth Street; investor page lists Sixth Street, Declaration Partners, Maverick Ventures, and SymphonyAI/SAI Group backing

One-Liner Description: AI Benefit-Risk Decision Engine; 24 specialized AI models, 100B biomedical datapoints, 50+ regulatory submissions supported. (web)
Target Segment: Pharma/biotech – Regulatory Affairs, Medical Affairs teams (web)
Key Capabilities: AI Benefit-Risk Decision Engine, 24 specialized AI models, auto-generated study reports/regulatory dossiers/HTA submissions with audit trails, 50+ regulatory submissions across 12 therapeutic areas (web)
Weaknesses: Focused on benefit-risk analysis and post-trial submissions; doesn't cover protocol design or site selection; Paris-based, early US expansion (web)
Funding Stage: $13.2M total; $7M seed (Sept 2025, led by The Moon Venture); previous €1.3M pre-seed (Plug & Play); €4M equity + Bpifrance (web)

One-Liner Description: Autonomous clinical system using AGI; automates protocol drafting, study design, data analysis, regulatory translation; $110M+ raised. (web)

Funding Stage: Series D $50M (late 2025, CDH Baifu, Xinding, HongShan); Series E $60M+ (Feb 2026, Trustar, Jinyi, Kaitai); $110M+ total raised (web)
